registry_description: Agent must query weather and AQI data for five cities and select the city with AQI ≤ 50 and today's high temperature in 15–26°C range choosing the lowest AQI among qualifying cities.
instruction: A friend is planning a weekend trip and wants to visit a city with good air quality and comfortable temperatures. Check the weather service at http://localhost:3000/ for all five available cities. Select the city that meets all of the following criteria: (1) Today's high temperature is between 15°C and 26°C (inclusive). (2) The AQI is at most 50. (3) Among cities that satisfy both conditions, choose the one with the lowest AQI. Save your recommendation to /workspace/output/travel_pick.json with the following keys: "city" (the city's URL slug, e.g., "shanghai"), "aqi" (integer), "temp_high_c" (integer), "reason" (one sentence explaining why this city was chosen).
mock_software_description: Weather mock service at http://localhost:3000/ pre-seeded with 5 cities accessible by URL slug. Seed data: harbin temp_high=12°C AQI=22 (fails temp 15–26°C); shanghai temp_high=22°C AQI=35 (qualifies); shenzhen temp_high=30°C AQI=28 (fails temp); beijing temp_high=28°C AQI=75 (fails AQI>50); chengdu temp_high=23°C AQI=120 (fails AQI>50). Only shanghai qualifies on all criteria. DB path: /tmp/weather.db. No external data required — all values are deterministic from seed constants.
scoring_criteria_overview: Three-dimension scoring. Dimension 1 (0.4): city slug in /workspace/output/travel_pick.json matches "shanghai". Dimension 2 (0.3): aqi == 35. Dimension 3 (0.3): temp_high_c within ±1 of 22. All three dimensions must be correct (score must equal 1.0) to pass; exit non-zero if score < 1.0.

registry_description: Agent must parse the hourly forecast for Shanghai and identify the longest continuous block of hours with zero precipitation suitable for an outdoor run.
instruction: You want to go for an outdoor run in Shanghai today. Check today's hourly forecast at http://localhost:3000/location/shanghai/hourly and identify the longest continuous block of hours where the precipitation reading is 0.0 (no rain). If there are multiple windows of the same length, choose the earliest one. Save your result to /workspace/output/exercise_window.json with the following keys: "start_hour" (integer 0–23), "end_hour" (integer 0–23 inclusive), "duration_hours" (integer count of hours in the window).
mock_software_description: Weather mock service at http://localhost:3000/ pre-seeded with 5 cities including Shanghai (slug: shanghai). Shanghai today: temp_high=22°C, temp_low=18°C, daily precip=4mm. Hourly temperature ranges 18–22°C all day. Hourly precipitation: ~0.36mm for hours 10–20 inclusive, zero outside that window. An hour is suitable when precipitation reading is 0.0 (no rain) — this is stated explicitly in the instruction. Ground-truth qualifying windows (zero precip): hours 0–9 (10 hours) and hours 21–23 (3 hours). Longest window: 0–9. DB path: /tmp/weather.db. No external data required — all values deterministic from seed constants.
scoring_criteria_overview: Three-dimension scoring. Check /workspace/output/exercise_window.json. Dimension 1 (0.4): start_hour == 0. Dimension 2 (0.4): end_hour == 9. Dimension 3 (0.2): duration_hours == 10 (must equal end_hour - start_hour + 1). All three dimensions must be correct (score must equal 1.0) to pass; exit non-zero if score < 1.0.
